sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: {"disease_related": [{"complication": "Hepatic decompensation (ascites, encephalopathy, variceal bleeding)", "management": "Manage portal hypertension (beta-blockers, endoscopic ligation, TIPS), diuretics, lactulose, protein restriction. Refer to hepatologist for transplant evaluation if appropriate."}, {"complication": "Portal vein tumor thrombosis", "management": "May be considered for locoregional therapy (TACE, TARE) in highly selected patients with segmental/lobar invasion. Systemic therapy is primary treatment."}, {"complication": "Hepatocellular rupture (rare)", "management": "Emergency embolization or surgery; stabilize then treat underlying HCC."}], "treatment_related": {"chemotherapy": ["Bone marrow suppression (neutropenia, thrombocytopenia)", "Mucositis", "Nausea/vomiting"], "immunotherapy": ["Immune-mediated adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies, dermatologic toxicity)", "Higher incidence of early death in first 6 months with ipilimumab+nivolumab (29% required high-dose steroids)"], "targeted_therapy": ["Hypertension, hand-foot skin reaction, diarrhea (sorafenib, lenvatinib, regorafenib, cabozantinib)", "Fatigue, proteinuria (bevacizumab, ramucirumab)", "Hepatotoxicity (especially in CTP B patients)"], "surgery": ["Post-hepatectomy liver failure (risk with inadequate future liver remnant)", "Bile leak, hemorrhage, infection", "Worsening of portal hypertension", "5-year recurrence rates >70% after resection"], "radiation": ["Radiation-induced liver disease (especially with CTP B/C or inadequate dosing constraints)", "Gastric ulcer (if tumor near stomach)", "Fatigue, nausea"]}}

cards.name: Immune-mediated adverse events
cards.detail: Immune-mediated adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies, dermatologic toxicity)

cards.name: Early death with ipilimumab+nivolumab
cards.detail: Higher incidence of early death in first 6 months with ipilimumab+nivolumab (29% required high-dose steroids)

cards.name: Hypertension, hand-foot skin reaction, diarrhea
cards.detail: Hypertension, hand-foot skin reaction, diarrhea (sorafenib, lenvatinib, regorafenib, cabozantinib)

cards.name: Post-hepatectomy liver failure
cards.detail: Post-hepatectomy liver failure (risk with inadequate future liver remnant)

cards.name: Radiation-induced liver disease
cards.detail: Radiation-induced liver disease (especially with CTP B/C or inadequate dosing constraints)
